title: Validate a deflate stream's internal state
notes: Returns true if the stream has a valid state. Production implementations check a magic number + status. In Rust we assume the borrow checker already guarantees validity, so any borrowable state is valid.

title: Validate an inflate stream's internal state
notes: Returns true if the stream has a valid state. See deflateStateCheck.

title: Allocation callback — returns a zeroed buffer
signature: pub fn zcalloc(opaque: usize, items: u32, size: u32) -> Vec<u8>
notes: C zlib's zcalloc(opaque, items, size) calls malloc(items*size). Rust equivalent: allocate a Vec<u8> of items*size bytes, zeroed.

title: Free callback — no-op in Rust
signature: pub fn zcfree(opaque: usize, ptr: Vec<u8>)
notes: C zlib's zcfree calls free(ptr). Taking Vec<u8> by value consumes it — Rust drops it automatically. No-op body.

title: Bit vector describing compile-time options
notes: Returns a bit vector encoding compile-time options: type sizes, optional features, operating system, compiler. The precise value is configuration-dependent; for a Rust port we can return 0 (all defaults).

title: Inject bits into the inflate stream's bit buffer
signature: pub fn inflatePrime(strm: &mut InflateStream, bits: i32, value: i32) -> Result<(), InflateError>
notes: Appends `bits` low bits of `value` to the stream's pre-existing bit buffer (state.hold / state.bits). Used to resume decompression at a known bit position. bits in [-1..16]; -1 clears the buffer.

title: Copy the deflate dictionary out of the state
signature: pub fn deflateGetDictionary(strm: &DeflateStream, dictionary: &mut [u8], dictLength: &mut usize) -> Result<(), DeflateError>
notes: Copies the current dictionary (from the sliding window) into `dictionary`. If dictionary is empty, just writes the length.

title: Preload a dictionary into the deflate state
signature: pub fn deflateSetDictionary(strm: &mut DeflateStream, dictionary: &[u8]) -> Result<(), DeflateError>
notes: Replaces the deflate stream's dictionary with the caller-provided bytes. Adler checksum is updated to match the new content.
